name: Terminal Surface
description: Rules for workflows executed through terminal commands, scripts, CLIs, local files, and command output validation.
whenToApply: The phase primarily uses shell commands, scripts, CLIs, package managers, or terminal output.
The phase validates a local file, generated artifact, or command result through terminal inspection.
whenNotToApply: The phase only uses browser or desktop app UI.
The terminal is only mentioned as a possible provider fallback rather than the actual workflow surface.
generationGuidance: Require commands to be scoped to the working directory and explain what observable output proves success.
Require validation through exit code, generated files, command output, or inspected artifacts.
Add user approval before destructive commands, credential changes, package installs, network uploads, or production-impacting operations.
Stop when required paths, tools, credentials, permissions, or command outputs are missing or ambiguous.
hardRequirements: Every terminal phase must include a validation command or observable command output.
Destructive shell actions must be represented as approval checkpoints.

runtimeReference.title: Terminal Surface Rules
runtimeReference.purpose: Terminal execution rules and common command automation mistakes.
whenToRead: Before executing any phase that uses terminal commands or scripts.
